full_response:
First write the Schwarzschild line element and keep only the   t–r  part  

ds² = −f(r) dt² + dr²/f(r) , with f(r) ≡ 1 − 2GM/r .    (1)

Introduce the tortoise coordinate r_* by  

dr_* ≡ dr / f(r) ⟹ dr = f(r) dr_* .    (2)

Substituting (2) into (1):

ds² = −f dt² + f dr_*² = f(r) [ −dt² + dr_*² ] .    (3)

Light-cone (null) coordinates are defined by  

u ≡ t − r_* , v ≡ t + r_* .    (4)

Their differentials are  

du = dt − dr_* , dv = dt + dr_*             ⇒         
dt = (du + dv)/2 , dr_* = (dv − du)/2 .    (5)

Compute −dt² + dr_*² :

−dt² + dr_*²  
 = −[(du + dv)/2]² + [(dv − du)/2]²  
 = −¼ (du² + 2 du dv + dv²) + ¼ (dv² − 2 du dv + du²)  
 = −du dv .    (6)

Insert (6) into (3):

ds² = f(r) ( −du dv ) = − [1 − 2GM/r(u,v)] du dv .    (7)

Thus, in the coordinates (u,v) the t–r sector of the metric has the form  

ds² = g_{uu} du² + 2g_{uv} du dv + g_{vv} dv² , with  

g_{uu} = 0 , g_{vv} = 0 , g_{uv} = g_{vu} = −½[1 − 2GM/r(u,v)] .    (8)

Answering the specific question:

\boxed{g_{uu}=0,\qquad g_{vv}=0}

Confidence: 85%
